fundamentals.name: Datadog, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Datadog, Inc. operates an observability and security platform for cloud applications in the United States and internationally. The company's products comprise infrastructure and application performance monitoring, log management, observability pipelines, synthetics, real user monitoring, product analytics, continuous profiler, database monitoring, data observability, LLM observability, error tracking, network monitoring, incident response, workflow automation and App builder, event management, bits AI SRE, cloud cost management, cloud security, code security, cloud SIEM, threat management, sensitive data scanner, and CI visibility. Datadog, Inc. was incorporated in 2010 and is headquartered in New York, New York.

judgment.summary: High-growth company with strong revenue and earnings expansion, but trades at a premium valuation and has thin operating margins.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimates by 18.3%; next earnings date is 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth 32.2% YoY and quarterly earnings growth 113.4% indicate strong top-line and bottom-line expansion.
Forward P/E of 78.41 is significantly lower than trailing P/E of 571.90, suggesting expected earnings growth.
Gross margin of 79.9% is very healthy, indicating efficient cost of goods sold.
Strong free cash flow of $936.73M TTM supports operations and potential investments.
Analyst consensus is 'strong_buy' with a mean target price near the current spot price.
negatives: Trailing P/E of 571.90 and Price/Sales of 21.62 indicate a very high valuation.
Operating margin of 0.8% is extremely thin, raising concerns about profitability despite high gross margins.
Return on assets of -0.2% and Return on equity of 3.9% are modest.
Debt/Equity ratio of 32.22 suggests significant leverage relative to equity.

news.paragraph: Datadog has hit a 52-week high and is flashing a golden cross signal, with multiple sources highlighting bullish Wall Street views and its trending status. The company's stock has been on a strong run, outpacing even some semiconductor stocks, as cyber-related equities gain momentum. With the next earnings date approaching on August 6th, the market is weighing whether this upward trajectory can be sustained.
